title: Salty Caramelized Brazilian Cumin Seeds Stir-fry
description: A salty and crisp brunch-friendly dish featuring cumin seeds in a flavorful vegan stir-fry that's perfect for adding a burst of flavor to your morning meal.

ingredients.name: 1 cup chopped onion
ingredients.notes: Finely chopped for better cooking and taste integration

ingredients.name: 1/4 cup sliced jalapeno peppers (optional, for heat)
ingredients.notes: Add a touch of heat if desired

ingredients.name: 3 cloves garlic, minced
ingredients.notes: For deeper umami flavors and aroma

ingredients.name: 2 cups mixed vegetables (e.g., bell peppers, zucchini, mushrooms, carrots, spinach)
ingredients.notes: To add variety and nutrients to the dish

ingredients.name: 1/4 cup tamari sauce
ingredients.notes: A gluten-free soy sauce alternative for a salty flavor base

ingredients.name: 1 teaspoon ground cumin seeds
ingredients.notes: For the characteristic Brazilian cumin taste

ingredients.name: Pinch of black pepper
ingredients.notes: To balance the flavors and enhance overall depth

instructions: Heat a pan over medium heat, add olive oil, then sauté onions until translucent.
Add minced garlic and jalapeno (if using) to cook for an additional minute. Stir frequently to prevent burning.
Toss in mixed vegetables and cumin seeds; stir well to coat evenly with spices.
Drizzle tamari sauce over the ingredients, then mix them together thoroughly.
Reduce heat to low and let everything simmer for about 5 minutes or until vegetables are tender but still crisp.
Season with sea salt to taste. Ensure seasoning is distributed evenly among all components.
Serve hot as a side dish or incorporate it into your favorite brunch creation.
